title: Digital Accounts After a Death: Where to Start
summary: Digital accounts are a newer, often overlooked category of estate work — a systematic approach makes it manageable.
category: After a Loss III: Estate & Digital
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Digital Accounts After a Death: Where to Start | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: Digital accounts are a newer, often overlooked category of estate work — a systematic approach makes it manageable.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Email is often the master key to resetting other accounts, so it's a high-priority first step.
A password manager, if the person used one, can save enormous time.
Not all platforms have the same process for family access.

paragraphs: Gaining access to the person's primary email account is often the most valuable single step, since it's frequently used to reset passwords on nearly every other account — banking, social media, subscriptions.

If the person used a password manager, locating the master password (sometimes in estate documents, sometimes known only to them) can unlock nearly everything else at once — worth prioritizing this search early.

nextSteps: Identify and prioritize access to the primary email account.
Search for any password manager and its master credentials.
Build a list of known digital accounts as you go, rather than trying to remember them all later.
disclaimer: General guidance — each platform's specific policy for deceased users' accounts varies.
